feature: DSOL v2: score-gated writeback with convergence check (ADR-098)
summary: Adds opt-in writeback job to self-improvement-loop workflow. New inputs: enable-writeback (boolean, default false, never via cron) and min-proposal-score (threshold for proposal filtering). Staleness detection via DRIFT_SELF_SCAN_FAILED environment variable when self-scan step fails. New helper scripts: scripts/check_dsol_convergence.py (convergence gate logic) and scripts/validate_proposals.py (proposal schema + score validation). ADR-098 documents the decision with status proposed. Blast reports stored in blast_reports/ as timestamped JSON artifacts.
design_goals: Never run writeback automatically via cron — opt-in only via workflow_dispatch
Score-Gate: drop proposals below min-proposal-score threshold before applying
Convergence-Gate: detect stagnation when identical proposals repeat across cycles
Staleness detection: annotate report when self-scan fails
All writeback logic isolated in dedicated workflow job with contents:write permission only
non_goals: No changes to scoring weights or thresholds
No autonomous push or PR creation — writeback is file-level, not git-level

self_analysis.notes: No signal logic changed; writeback is a workflow-level operational feature only.
